instructions: Rewrite `docs/Jitsu Architecture Overview.md` to accurately reflect the current system architecture. You must document the pipeline: Layer 1 (Domain Models with `TargetResolutionMode`), Layer 1.5 (ContextCompiler with AST-First/AUTO fallback and Context Manifests), Layer 2 (Providers), and Layer 3 (MCP Server with `jitsu_request_context` for progressive disclosure). The code is the source of truth; ensure the document perfectly describes how the system saves tokens and shifts preparation to Python.
anti_patterns: Do not hallucinate features that are not present in the code context.
Do not delete historical context from older docs if it explains the 'why', but ensure the 'how' reflects the current codebase.
completion_criteria: The Architecture Overview accurately explains AST-first handoff, AUTO mode, and Progressive Disclosure.

instructions: Rewrite the `README.md` to serve as a modern, accurate front-page for the Jitsu project. Highlight its core value proposition: an AST-first, strictly-typed AI orchestrator that uses Definition of Done enforcement and Progressive Disclosure to prevent 'Context Drift' in LLM agents. Provide clear instructions on how to use `uv run jitsu submit` to feed it epics.
anti_patterns: Do not make the README overly verbose; keep it punchy, developer-focused, and easy to read.
completion_criteria: The README explains what Jitsu is, why it exists (Context Drift prevention), and how to run it.
